subject: Renamed policy-drools docker image
commit_message:
Renamed policy-drools docker image

Renamed from:
onap/policy/policy-drools
to
onap/policy-drools
to enable consistent naming across nexus and docker hub

Issue-ID: POLICY-674
Change-Id: Ib0b9df97f398c5c1715f736f11f6481cf6d8427f
Signed-off-by: mmis <michael.morris@ericsson.com>

entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Verified-1

Build Failed 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/policy-drools-pdp-master-verify-java/368/ : FAILURE

Unable to build due to a non-resolvable parent POM. Search logs for "[FATAL] Non-resolvable parent POM" to find the logs.

The fix for this is usually to make sure your poms are pointing to parents that exist. Double check:

 * groupId is correct and no typos
 * artifactId is correct and no typos
 * version is correct for branch ( https://jenkins.onap.org/job/policy-drools-pdp-master-verify-java/368/ )

Build failed to find artifact org.onap.dmaap.messagerouter.dmaapclient:dmaapClient:jar:1.1.3-SNAPSHOT needed for sub-project org.onap.policy.drools-pdp:policy-endpoints:jar:1.2.0-SNAPSHOT. Check correct groupId, artifactId and version. This may also indicate a problem with nexus. Another possibility is that this is a -SNAPSHOT version and it has expired from nexus -- in that case check with the project which provides the artifact. ( https://jenkins.onap.org/job/policy-drools-pdp-master-verify-java/368/ )

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/policy-drools-pdp-master-verify-java/368
Patch Set 2: Patch Set 1 was rebased

Patch Set 2: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/policy-drools-pdp-master-verify-java/369/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/policy-drools-pdp-master-verify-java/369

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

What is the benefit of doing this name change?   

There are side effects for those doing testing in our team and integration teams.   OOM and integration source code repos I think have occurrences and may need changes as well. I think there are references to default naming.

Daniel Cruz in our team and integration teams need to be aware, so they are not picking up stale images on their testing.   We need to make sure that the change is socialized with testing teams so they don't test with old images, if they don't do appropriate changes in their lab configurations.   It may be better to do these changes in the beginning of a release.

What activity is publishing to github?   May be somebody that have been experimenting.  I only see v1.1.0 and v1.1.1 so it may have been a short lived experiment with wrong scripts somehow and somewhere.   It does not seem clear to me that these scripts are the source of the problem, nor I understand why those images have been pushed to dockerhub.

Patch Set 2:

The dockerhub images I see were pushed 4 months ago ..

Patch Set 2:

Snae issue exists with msb images, they get prepended  "msb-".   The script that is publishing to dockerhub probably flattens >2 level directory style naming hierarchies into 2-level ones >2 ie. in onap  "onap/y/z" to "onap/y-z" blindly for dockerhub.

Are there naming restrictions in dockerhub?   In any case, dockerhub, doesn't even have the official amsterdam release which is v1.1.3.   

The change to have unified naming structure makes sense if we officially are indeed required to change to dockerhub, but it would be much better to do this at the very beginning of a release, not towards the end of M4.

Patch Set 2: -Code-Review

Daniel, please follow up with this review and similars, if/when integrated you would have to modify windriver lab under /opt/policy docker-compose to pick up the name image change to work with the latest snapshot image.    See Michael's review in docker:   https://gerrit.onap.org/r/#/c/35779/ that you'll have to pick up.
Change has been successfully merged by Pamela Dragosh

Patch Set 2:

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/policy-drools-pdp-master-merge-scm-mvn-script/10/ : SUCCESS
